activities.title: International Volunteers
activities.description: Contribute to ecotourism expeditions with international volunteers. Help highlight nature’s potential through sustainable actions!
activities.descriptionComplete:
Here is the information to join as an international volunteer at Terrae Basecamp via the Worldpackers app:

Requirements:
- Minimum stay of 1 week
- Daily fee: 20 USD
- Minimum age: 18 years

Provided facilities:
- Accommodation in a traditional house
- Pick-up and drop-off service
- Three meals a day
- Opportunity to expand your network
- E-Certificate
- Travel experience to breathtaking natural destinations

To start your volunteer, click the Register button on this page!

activities.title: Domestic Volunteers
activities.description: Join as a domestic volunteer and contribute to ecotourism expeditions. Help showcase nature’s potential through sustainable real-world actions!
activities.descriptionComplete:
Here is the information to join as a local volunteer at Terrae Basecamp:

Requirements:
- Minimum age: 18 years old
- Fee: Rp 150,000/pax
- Program duration: 2 days 1 night

Facilities provided:
- Homestay (Private Room)
- Breakfast
- Coffee break
- Waterfall expedition
- Tourism discussion
- Networking
- E-Certificate

To start your volunteer, click the Register button on this page!

projects.sectionDescription: At Terrae, we empower local communities through sustainable ecotourism, aligning with global Sustainable Development Goals. Our projects create jobs, support community resilience, and promote environmental conservation. By involving local residents in tourism, we help reduce poverty (SDG 1), foster sustainable communities (SDG 11), and contribute to climate action (SDG 13).
projects.sectionExploreText: Explore our initiatives and be part of the change!

projects.title: Ecotourism
projects.description: Terrae is an ecotourism destination that offers a cultural immersion experience with remote village communities in the tropical forests near Bromo Tengger Semeru National Park. Tourists can enjoy the beauty of nature, explore various destinations such as waterfalls and mountains, and experience an authentic local lifestyle. Additionally, Terrae also focuses on processing local products as part of a sustainable tourism experience.

projects.title: Environment
projects.description: As part of its commitment to sustainability, Terrae promotes environmental conservation through various ecotourism programs. By maintaining a balance between tourism and nature, Terrae supports responsible natural resource management and encourages eco-friendly practices. Through these initiatives, Terrae strives to preserve the surrounding ecosystem and raise awareness of the importance of conservation.

projects.title: Social
projects.description: Terrae is not just a tourist destination but also a platform for empowering local communities. By involving residents as tour guides, food providers, and transportation service operators, Terrae creates job opportunities and improves the economic well-being of the surrounding community. Additionally, Terrae has received a tourism management certification from the Netherlands' Programma Uitzending Managers in recognition of its contribution to the development of sustainable and culturally-based village tourism in Java.

items.name: Lina Rischawy
items.shortText: I’m Lina, the first volunteer on the team, and I’m truly impressed by what this group aims to achieve. This is the right approach—our future depends on the youth, and our planet is on the brink of irreversible destruction. We take Earth for granted, polluting it with waste, deforestation, and thoughtless actions.
items.fullText:
In Germany, we've protested for over 40 years for environmental protection, but climate change is nearly unstoppable. Everyone must contribute, but the government must act too. In our country, excessive plastic packaging and plastic bags are banned—people bring fabric bags. A plastic tax or recycled cardboard packaging could be solutions.
Another major issue is waste disposal. Why is it so dirty despite Indonesia’s natural and cultural beauty? Where are the trash cans, waste collection, and recycling programs? Education in schools, awareness in villages, and media campaigns are crucial.
Terrae Volunteer is stepping in where the government has failed, and that deserves support. During my time here, we collected waste, raised awareness, and explored solutions for waste management.

guildhouseCTA.title:
Don't Understand How to 
Take the Job?
guildhouseCTA.description: Press the button below to learn more about how to take jobs listed in the quests list above.

guildhouseCTA.title: Not Registered Yet?
guildhouseCTA.description:
Sign up for the Guild House now and contribute to the community to help others, all while earning fair wages and supporting each other.
Plus, enjoy the benefits of being a guild member.

benefits.title: Play to Earn
benefits.description:
Take on missions, complete them, earn rewards—no pressure, just fun!
Enjoy the results of your hard work.

benefits.title: Free Training
benefits.description: Get free English training with international mentors who will help you improve your English skills.

benefits.title: Free Pickup Service
benefits.description:
Get subsidized pickup services to your job location, saving you
hassle and cost of commuting. Focus your energy on the work itself.
(Available only in the Senduro area).

recruitment.title: Join Us
recruitment.description: We are opening opportunities for you!

cards.title: Volunteer
cards.description: Contribute to ecotourism expeditions alongside international volunteers. Help showcase nature’s potential through sustainable real-world actions!

cards.title: Guild Member
cards.description: Take on quests, complete tasks, and help the community! From cleaning the environment to assisting the elderly, every mission supports sustainable ecotourism.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: How do I register as a volunteer?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer: Prospective volunteers must register through the Worldpackers application before joining the program. This ensures official registration and access to program details.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: Is there a fee to participate in the program?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer: Yes, volunteers must contribute $20 per person per day to support operational costs and community contributions.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: What is the contribution payment system?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer:
• 50% deposit due by the first day of briefing.
• Remaining 50% payable halfway through the stay.
• Payments via e-banking transfer or cash on-site.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: What facilities are provided to volunteers?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer:
• Accommodation in a traditional local home.
• Three daily meals.
• Shuttle transport within Senduro, including free pick-up from Klakah Station (07:00 - 17:00 WIB).
• Eco-tourism and cultural experiences.
• E-Certificate upon completion.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: How long is the volunteer program?
Is there an age limit to join?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer: Participants must be at least 18 years. Those under 18 require parental permission.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: What is prohibited during the program?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer:
• No alcohol, narcotics, or illegal substances.
• No discrimination, harassment, or violence.
• No damage to flora and fauna.
• No leaving the program area without permission.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: What happens if I violate the rules?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer:
• Minor violations: Verbal warning.
• Serious violations: Possible expulsion without refund.
• Legal matters may be reported to authorities.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: What is the cancellation and refund policy?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer:
• Before arrival: Non-refundable deposit, except in emergencies.
• After arrival: No refunds unless the program is canceled by Terrae.

faq-terraevolunteers.question: Who can I contact in case of emergency?
faq-terraevolunteers.answer: Contact the program coordinator via the number provided upon arrival.

faq-guildhouse.question: How do I register as a freelance worker?
faq-guildhouse.answer: Prospective freelance workers must register through the Guild House platform before joining. This ensures all applicants are officially recorded and can access job opportunities.

faq-guildhouse.question: Is there a fee to participate as a freelance worker?
faq-guildhouse.answer: No, there is no registration fee to become a freelance worker at Terrae Guild House.

faq-guildhouse.question: What is the payment system for freelance workers?
faq-guildhouse.answer: Freelancers receive payments based on a commission system. The commission amount is tentative and depends on the type of work performed.

faq-guildhouse.question: What facilities are provided to freelance workers?
faq-guildhouse.answer:
• Pick-up and drop-off service (availability depends on the area).
• Opportunity to work in a dynamic environment with eco-tourism and community-based projects.

faq-guildhouse.question: How long is the freelance work duration?
faq-guildhouse.answer: The duration depends on job availability and agreements between the worker and Terrae Guild House. Freelancers can take on multiple assignments based on their availability.

faq-guildhouse.question: Is there an age limit to join as a freelance worker?
faq-guildhouse.answer: Yes, applicants must be at least 18 years.

faq-guildhouse.question: What is prohibited during the work period?
faq-guildhouse.answer:
• Engaging in unprofessional behavior, harassment, or discrimination.
• Failing to complete assigned tasks responsibly.
• Violating company policies or local regulations.

faq-guildhouse.question: What happens if I violate the rules?
faq-guildhouse.answer:
• Minor violations: Warning.
• Serious violations: Possible termination of freelance work opportunities.
• Legal violations may be reported to the authorities.

faq-guildhouse.question: What is the cancellation policy for freelance workers?
faq-guildhouse.answer: If a freelancer cannot complete an assigned job, they must notify the coordinator as soon as possible. Repeated cancellations without valid reasons may result in removal from future job opportunities.

faq-guildhouse.question: Who can I contact in case of emergency?
faq-guildhouse.answer: Freelance workers can contact the program coordinator via the provided contact number upon assignment.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Registration Terms and Conditions
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Registration: Must be done via the Worldpackers app.
Contribution: $20 per day per person for operational costs.

Payment System:
• 50% deposit on the first day.
• Remaining 50% after half of the stay.
• Payment via e-banking or cash.

Duration: 1 to 12 weeks.
Minimum Age: 18+ or with parental consent.
Health: Complete health form before arrival.
Attitude and Adaptation: Open to local culture and willing to adapt.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Provided Facilities
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Accommodation: Traditional local house.
Meals: Three meals a day, prepared by locals.

Transportation:
• Shuttle service within Senduro.
• Free pickup from Klakah Station (07:00 - 17:00 WIB).

Nature Tourism: Eco-tourism and cultural experiences.
Certificate: E-Certificate upon completion.
Social Network: Connect with local and international volunteers.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Program Schedule
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Day One: Introduction and Adaptation
• Program briefing and rules introduction.
• Meet locals and learn about culture.
• Discuss program and align goals.
• Rest and recover from the journey.

Day Two and Beyond: Flexible Schedule
• Activities based on ongoing projects.
• 4 flexible working hours per day.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Volunteer Responsibilities
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Respecting Culture: Honor local traditions and norms.
Active Participation: Engage in program activities.
Cleanliness & Environment: Maintain hygiene and sustainability.
Behavior Ethics: Avoid harming local communities.
Reporting: Report issues to the coordinator.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Prohibited Activities
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Prohibited Substances: No alcohol, narcotics, or illegal substances.
Discrimination & Violence: Zero tolerance.
Environmental Damage: No harming flora or fauna.
Leaving the Area: No leaving without permission.

coc-terraevolunteers.question: Sanctions and Consequences
coc-terraevolunteers.answer:
Minor Violations: Verbal warning.
Major Violations: Expulsion without refund.
Legal Action: Serious cases reported to authorities.

coc-guildhouse.question: Registration Terms and Conditions
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Registration: Via Guild House section.
Minimum Age: 18 years old.
Education: High school diploma or equivalent.
Skills: Good communication and responsibility.

coc-guildhouse.question: Provided Facilities
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Transportation: Pickup/drop-off service based on location.
Commission: Payment based on task and performance.

coc-guildhouse.question: Work Schedule
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Schedules assigned via Guild House platform.
Freelancers must meet deadlines and report issues promptly.

coc-guildhouse.question: Freelance Worker Responsibilities
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Professionalism: Maintain responsibility.
Respecting Culture: Follow workplace ethics.
Quality Work: Ensure accuracy.
Communication: Report progress.

coc-guildhouse.question: Prohibited Activities
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Prohibited Substances: No alcohol/drugs.
Discrimination & Violence: Not tolerated.
Unprofessional Behavior: No disruptive conduct.
Confidentiality Breach: No leaking info.

coc-guildhouse.question: Sanctions and Consequences
coc-guildhouse.answer:
Minor Violations: Verbal warnings from the coordinator.
Major Violations: Termination of the freelance contract.
Legal Action: Serious offenses will be reported to the authorities.
